target.title: fix(cron): auto-migrate legacy cron store
target.body:
Fixes #90072

## Summary

- Add a non-interactive cron auto-migration path that runs during doctor config preflight.
- Merge legacy JSON-only cron jobs into the SQLite cron store before runtime reads cron rows, while keeping existing SQLite rows as the winner for duplicate job ids.
- Archive legacy cron store files only after the SQLite write succeeds, and include default/custom legacy cron stores in lightweight startup detection for read-only preflight paths.
- Run the shared CLI bootstrap on the optimized `gateway run` fast path so gateway startup gets the same migration preflight as the regular CLI path.

## Root Cause

Cron runtime now reads and writes the canonical SQLite-backed store only. The existing doctor cron repair flow could import legacy `jobs.json` / `jobs-state.json`, but ordinary startup/preflight paths did not call that cron migration before `gateway` or `cron list` touched runtime state. The optimized `gateway run` fast path also skipped the shared command bootstrap, so gateway startup could still bypass the new preflight migration until this PR routed that fast path through the same bootstrap.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/cron/auto-migration.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.state-migration.test.ts src/cli/program/config-guard.test.ts src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/cli/run-main.test.ts`
- `node /private/tmp/openclaw-cron-upgrade-proof.mjs`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --codex-bin /private/tmp/codex-autoreview-flex` (one allowed draft-PR autoreview round, clean before the follow-up fast-path fix; skipped a second autoreview per local draft-PR guardrail)

GitHub CI: after pushing head `2bee816deb1e7a9f4cb12419cab67e9a91583bd9`, the GitHub connector returned no workflow runs yet for that SHA. Older head `7b2637e0a0a49b68e7372a1be8559d2f7dca61ec` had Workflow Sanity success and the broad CI/CodeQL/OpenGrep runs skipped while the PR was draft/fork; no failing CI was found.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: legacy cron jobs are migrated into SQLite during config preflight before gateway startup, `cron status`, and `cron list` rely on the SQLite-only cron store.

Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout using a throwaway `OPENCLAW_HOME`, real loopback Gateway, real `openclaw.mjs` CLI entrypoint, and a representative legacy cron store with `jobs.json`, split `jobs-state.json`, and a legacy JSONL run log. The temp home path was redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node /private/tmp/openclaw-cron-upgrade-proof.mjs`. The script prebuilt once with `node scripts/run-node.mjs --version`, then used `openclaw.mjs` to start `gateway run --allow-unconfigured --auth none --bind loopback --port <temp>`, call `cron status --json`, call `cron list --all --json`, query `state/openclaw.sqlite`, and read `cron runs --id legacy-due-every`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "before": {
    "legacyJobsJsonCount": 3,
    "legacyJobsStateCount": 3,
    "sqliteCronJobsRows": 0,
    "sqliteCronRunLogRows": 0
  },
  "after": {
    "cronStatus": {
      "enabled": true,
      "jobs": 3,
      "nextWakeAtMs": 1780586957707
    },
    "cronListAll": [
      {
        "id": "legacy-due-every",
        "enabled": true,
        "scheduleKind": "every",
        "lastRunStatus": "ok"
      },
      {
        "id": "legacy-future-at",
        "enabled": true,
        "scheduleKind": "at",
        "nextRunAtMs": 4070908800000,
        "deliveryMode": "webhook"
      },
      {
        "id": "legacy-disabled-past",
        "enabled": false,
        "scheduleKind": "at",
        "deliveryMode": "webhook"
      }
    ],
    "sqlite": {
      "cronJobs": 3,
      "cronRunLogs": 2,
      "jobs": [
        { "jobId": "legacy-disabled-past", "enabled": 0, "scheduleKind": "at", "runningAtMs": null },
        { "jobId": "legacy-due-every", "enabled": 1, "scheduleKind": "every", "runningAtMs": null, "lastRunStatus": "ok" },
        { "jobId": "legacy-future-at", "enabled": 1, "scheduleKind": "at", "runningAtMs": null }
      ]
    },
    "archives": {
      "jobsJsonMigrated": true,
      "jobsStateMigrated": true,
      "legacyRunLogMigrated": true
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: the three legacy jobs became three SQLite `cron_jobs` rows before the gateway cron runtime/list surfaces read the store. `cron status` reported `jobs: 3`, `cron list --all` returned all three migrated jobs, legacy `notify: true` rows were converted to webhook delivery, and the old `jobs.json`, `jobs-state.json`, and legacy run-log file were archived after durable SQLite writes.

Observed catch-up behavior: the overdue recurring legacy job was caught up once on gateway startup, completed with `lastRunStatus: "ok"`, advanced `nextRunAtMs`, and left `runningAtMs: null`. The SQLite run-log count was `2`: one imported legacy row plus one startup catch-up row.

What was not tested: a packaged 2026.5.28-to-PR-head install upgrade with a real user's cron store. The proof used a representative legacy JSON/state/run-log fixture in an isolated temp home, not production user data.
target.author: monkeyleet
